title: Web material
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a web of plant material, an aerosol-forming article e.g. a consumable for use in a smoking substitute system and particularly, although not exclusively, to a heat-not-burn (HNB) consumable, wherein the article/consumable comprises a gathered web of plant material. The present disclosure also relates to a method of forming the web of plant material. The web of plant material (e.g. tobacco) has a plurality of longitudinally-extending shreds of plant material and there is a plurality of transverse bridge portions each extending between and joining two or more of the shreds. None of the bridge portions extend across the entire transverse width of the web. Adjacent bridge portions are preferably longitudinally spaced from one another such that they are completely unaligned in the transverse direction.
claims: Claims Claims: 1. A web of plant material having a plurality of longitudinally-extending shreds of plant material wherein there is a plurality of transverse bridge portions each extending between and joining two or more of the shreds, wherein none of the bridge portions extend across the entire transverse width of the web. 2. A web according to claim 1 wherein adjacent bridge portions are longitudinally spaced from one another. 3. A web according to claim 2 wherein adjacent transverse bridge portions are completely unaligned in the transverse direction. 4. A web according to any one of the preceding claims wherein each transverse bridge portion spans ten or fewer immediately adjacent shreds of plant material. 5. A web according to any one of the preceding claims wherein, upon progression along the transverse direction, adjacent bridge portions get progressively closer to one of the lateral ends of the web such that the transverse bridge portions are diagonally arranged/offset on the web of plant material. 6. A web according to any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein each of the plurality of transverse bridge portions is closer to one of the lateral ends of the web than its two adjacent bridge portions. 7. A web according to any one of the preceding claims having a plurality of longitudinally-extending discontinuous slits dividing the plant material into the plurality of longitudinally-extending shreds, each discontinuous slits comprising a respective discontinuity forming the respective transverse bridge portion between adjacent shreds. 8. A web according to claim 7 comprising a first group of discontinuous slits wherein all transverse bridge portions within the first group are longitudinally spaced from one another. 9. A web according to claim 8 further comprising a second group of discontinuous slits transversely spaced from the first group of discontinuous slits by a group of continuous slits. 10. An aerosol-forming article comprising an aerosol-forming substrate comprising a web of plant material according to any one of the preceding claims, the web being gathered and at least partially circumscribed by a wrapping layer. 1 1. An article according to claim 10 wherein the article is a heat-not-burn (HNB) consumable. 12. A smoking substitute system comprising an aerosol-forming article according to claim 10 or 1 1 and a device comprising a heating element. 13. A smoking substitute system according to claim 12, wherein the heating element is elongate and is configured to penetrate the aerosol-forming substrate of the aerosol-forming article. 14. A smoking substitute system according to claim 12, wherein the device and the aerosol-forming article are configured to permit insertion of the aerosol-forming article into a cavity in the device, and wherein said heating element is elongate and configured to penetrate the aerosol-forming substrate upon insertion of the aerosol-forming article into said cavity. 15. A smoking substitute system according to claim 14, wherein said heating element projects into said cavity so as to penetrate the aerosol-forming substrate upon insertion of said aerosol-forming article into said cavity. 16. A method of using the system according to any one of claims 12 to 15, the method comprising: inserting the article into the device; and heating the article using the heating element. 17. A method of forming a web of plant material comprising: providing a sheet of plant material; dividing the plant material into a plurality of longitudinally-extending shreds of plant material by forming a plurality longitudinally-extending slits, wherein the slits are discontinuous such that the method further comprises leaving a plurality of unslit transverse bridge portions each joining at least two adjacent shreds, wherein none of the bridge portions extend across the entire transverse width of the web. 18. A method according to claim 17 comprising leaving un-slit transverse bridge portions such that adjacent transverse bridge portions are longitudinally spaced relative to one another. 19. A method according to claim 17 or claim 18 comprising forming a first group of discontinuous slits and transverse bridge portions by passing the sheet of plant material through a pair of interdigitated transverse stacks of rotary cutting blades wherein a plurality of blades within the interdigitated transverse stacks each comprise a respective notch, wherein the notches are angularly spaced in a circumferential direction relative to one another. 20. A method according to claim 19 comprising using a pair of interdigitated transverse stacks further comprising at least one un-notched blade for forming a continuous slit in the web of plant material. 21. A method according to claim 19 or claim 20 comprising using a pair of interdigitated transverse stacks wherein the plurality of notched blades are arranged within the interdigitated transverse stacks such that the angular spacing from a notch in a first blade within the interdigitated transverse stack progressively increases in the stacking direction of the blades. 22. A method according to any one of claims 17 to 20 comprising using a pair of interdigitated transverse stacks wherein the plurality of notched blades are arranged within the interdigitated transverse stacks such that the angular spacing from a notch in a first blade within the interdigitated transverse stack alternately increases and decreases in the stacking direction of the blades. 23. A method of forming an article according to claim 10 or claim 1 1 comprising: forming the web of plant material using the method according to any one of claims 17 to 22; gathering the web of plant material to form a cylindrical rod; cutting the cylindrical rod to form a cylindrical aerosol-forming substrate; and circumscribing the aerosol-forming substrate using a wrapping layer.
